foreground.h(3) Library Functions Manual foreground.h(3)

NAME

foreground.h

SYNOPSIS

Functions


char * image_print (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_256color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_color_utf8 (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t *image, bool use_background, const char *palette)

Function Documentation

char * image_print (const image_t * p, const char * palette)

Definition at line 27 of file foreground.c.

27                                                          {
28   if (!p || !palette) {
29     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
30     return NULL;
31   }
32   if (!p->pixels) {
33     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p->pixels is NULL");
34     return NULL;
35   }
36 
37   const int h = p->h;
38   const int w = p->w;
39 
40   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
41     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
42     return NULL;
43   }
44 
45   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
46   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
47   if (!utf8_cache) {
48     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar rendering");
49     return NULL;
50   }
51 
52   // Character index ramp is now part of UTF-8 cache - no separate cache needed
53 
54   // Need space for h rows with UTF-8 characters, plus h-1 newlines, plus null terminator
55   const size_t max_char_bytes = 4; // Max UTF-8 character size
56 
57   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
58 
59   // Use outbuf_t for efficient UTF-8 RLE emission (same as SIMD renderers)
60   outbuf_t ob = {0};
61 
62   // Calculate buffer size with overflow checking
63   size_t w_times_bytes;
64   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)w, max_char_bytes, &w_times_bytes) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
65     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width too large for UTF-8 encoding");
66     return NULL;
67   }
68 
69   size_t w_times_bytes_plus_one;
70   if (checked_size_add(w_times_bytes, 1, &w_times_bytes_plus_one) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
71     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width * bytes + 1 overflow");
72     return NULL;
73   }
74 
75   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, w_times_bytes_plus_one, &ob.cap) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
76     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: height * (width * bytes + 1) overflow");
77     return NULL;
78   }
79 
80   ob.buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ob.cap ? ob.cap : 1, char *);
81   if (!ob.buf) {
82     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate output buffer for scalar rendering");
83     return NULL;
84   }
85 
86   // Process pixels with UTF-8 RLE emission (same approach as SIMD)
87   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
88     const int row_offset = y * w;
89 
90     for (int x = 0; x < w;) {
91       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
92       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
93       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
94 
95       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
96       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
97       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
98       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
99 
100       // Use same 64-entry cache as SIMD for consistency
101       // Use char_idx to properly index into cache64, not luma_idx
102       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache64[char_idx];
103 
104       // Find run length for same character (RLE optimization)
105       int j = x + 1;
106       while (j < w) {
107         const rgb_pixel_t next_pixel = pix[row_offset + j];
108         const int next_luminance = (77 * next_pixel.r + 150 * next_pixel.g + 29 * next_pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
109         uint8_t next_safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(next_luminance);
110         uint8_t next_luma_idx = (uint8_t)(next_safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
111         uint8_t next_char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[next_luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
112         if (next_char_idx != char_idx)
113           break;
114         j++;
115       }
116       uint32_t run = (uint32_t)(j - x);
117 
118       // Emit UTF-8 character with RLE (same as SIMD)
119       ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
120       if (rep_is_profitable(run)) {
121         emit_rep(&ob, run - 1);
122       } else {
123         for (uint32_t k = 1; k < run; k++) {
124           ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
125         }
126       }
127       x = j;
128     }
129 
130     // Add newline between rows (except last row)
131     if (y != h - 1) {
132       ob_putc(&ob, '\n');
133     }
134   }
135 
136   ob_term(&ob);
137   return ob.buf;
138 }








char
  * image_print_16color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 535 of file foreground.c.


535                                                                      {
536   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
537     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
538     return NULL;
539   }
540 
541   int h = image->h;
542   int w = image->w;
543 
544   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
545     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
546     return NULL;
547   }
548 
549   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
550   ansi_fast_init_16color();
551 
552   // Calculate buffer size (smaller than 256-color due to shorter ANSI sequences)
553   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
554   // Calculate with overflow checking
555   size_t h_times_w;
556   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
557     return NULL;
558   }
559 
560   size_t h_times_w_times_12;
561   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 12u, &h_times_w_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
562     return NULL;
563   }
564 
565   size_t buffer_size;
566   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
567     return NULL;
568   }
569 
570   char *buffer;
571   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
572 
573   char *ptr = buffer;
574   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
575 
576   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
577   if (!utf8_cache) {
578     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color rendering");
579     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
580     return NULL;
581   }
582 
583   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
584     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
585       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
586 
587       // Convert RGB to 16-color index and generate ANSI sequence
588       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
589       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
590 
591       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
592       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
593 
594       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
595       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
596       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
597       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
598 
599       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[char_idx];
600 
601       if (char_info) {
602         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
603         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
604           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
605         }
606       } else {
607         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
608         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
609         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
610         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
611       }
612     }
613 
614     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
615     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
616     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
617     if (y < h - 1) {
618       *ptr++ = '\n';
619     }
620   }
621 
622   *ptr = '\0';
623   return buffer;
624 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 650 of file foreground.c.


650                                                                               {
651   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
652     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
653     return NULL;
654   }
655 
656   int h = image->h;
657   int w = image->w;
658 
659   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
660     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
661     return NULL;
662   }
663 
664   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
665   ansi_fast_init_16color();
666 
667   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
668   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
669   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
670   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
671 
672   // Calculate buffer size (same as non-dithered version)
673   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
674   // Calculate with overflow checking
675   size_t h_times_w2;
676   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w2) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
677     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
678     return NULL;
679   }
680 
681   size_t h_times_w2_times_12;
682   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w2, 12u, &h_times_w2_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
683     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
684     return NULL;
685   }
686 
687   size_t buffer_size;
688   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w2_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
689     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
690     return NULL;
691   }
692 
693   char *buffer;
694   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
695 
696   char *ptr = buffer;
697   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
698 
699   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
700   if (!utf8_cache) {
701     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered rendering");
702     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
703     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
704     return NULL;
705   }
706 
707   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
708     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
709       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
710 
711       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
712       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
713       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
714 
715       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
716       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
717 
718       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
719       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
720       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
721       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
722 
723       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[char_idx];
724 
725       if (char_info) {
726         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
727         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
728           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
729         }
730       } else {
731         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
732         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
733         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
734         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
735       }
736     }
737 
738     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
739     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
740     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
741     if (y < h - 1) {
742       *ptr++ = '\n';
743     }
744   }
745 
746   *ptr = '\0';
747   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
748   return buffer;
749 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t * image,
  bool use_background, const char * palette)


Definition at line 752 of file foreground.c.


752                                                                                                                    {
753   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
754     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
755     return NULL;
756   }
757 
758   int h = image->h;
759   int w = image->w;
760 
761   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
762     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered_with_background: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
763     return NULL;
764   }
765 
766   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
767   ansi_fast_init_16color();
768 
769   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
770   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
771   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
772   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
773 
774   // Calculate buffer size (larger for background mode due to more ANSI sequences)
775   size_t buffer_size =
776       (size_t)h * (size_t)w * (use_background ? 24 : 12) + (size_t)h; // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
777   char *buffer;
778   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
779 
780   char *ptr = buffer;
781   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
782 
783   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
784   if (!utf8_cache) {
785     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered bg rendering");
786     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
787     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
788     return NULL;
789   }
790 
791   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
792     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
793       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
794 
795       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
796       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
797 
798       if (use_background) {
799         // Background mode: use contrasting foreground on background color
800         uint8_t bg_r, bg_g, bg_b;
801         get_16color_rgb(color_index, &bg_r, &bg_g, &bg_b);
802 
803         // Calculate luminance to choose contrasting foreground
804         int bg_luminance = (bg_r * 77 + bg_g * 150 + bg_b * 29) / 256;
805         uint8_t fg_color = (bg_luminance < 127) ? 15 : 0; // White on dark, black on bright
806 
807         ptr = append_16color_bg(ptr, color_index); // Set background to pixel color
808         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, fg_color);    // Set contrasting foreground
809       } else {
810         // Foreground mode: set foreground to pixel color
811         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
812       }
813 
814       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
815       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
816 
817       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
818       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
819 
820       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
821 
822       if (char_info) {
823         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
824         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
825           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
826         }
827       } else {
828         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
829         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
830         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
831         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
832       }
833     }
834 
835     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
836     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
837     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
838     if (y < h - 1) {
839       *ptr++ = '\n';
840     }
841   }
842 
843   *ptr = '\0';
844   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
845   return buffer;
846 }










char
  * image_print_256color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 433 of file foreground.c.


433                                                                       {
434   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
435     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
436     return NULL;
437   }
438 
439   int h = image->h;
440   int w = image->w;
441 
442   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
443     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_256color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
444     return NULL;
445   }
446 
447   ansi_fast_init_256color();
448 
449   // Buffer size: 256-color ANSI codes are "\033[38;5;NNNm" (max 13 bytes) + char + reset + newline
450   size_t h_times_w;
451   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
452     return NULL;
453   }
454 
455   size_t h_times_w_times_18;
456   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 18u, &h_times_w_times_18) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
457     return NULL;
458   }
459 
460   size_t buffer_size;
461   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_18, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
462     return NULL;
463   }
464 
465   char *buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
466   char *ptr = buffer;
467   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
468 
469   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
470   if (!utf8_cache) {
471     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 256-color rendering");
472     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
473     return NULL;
474   }
475 
476   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
477     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
478       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
479 
480       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_256color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
481       ptr = append_256color_fg(ptr, color_index);
482 
483       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
484 
485       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
486 
487       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
488 
489       if (char_info && char_info->byte_len > 0) {
490         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
491           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
492         }
493       } else {
494         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
495         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
496         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
497       }
498     }
499 
500     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
501     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
502     if (y < h - 1) {
503       *ptr++ = '\n';
504     }
505   }
506 
507   *ptr = '\0';
508   return buffer;
509 }










char
  * image_print_color (const image_t * p, const char * palette)


Converts an image to colored ASCII art with ANSI escape codes.




This function generates a string representation of an image where
    each pixel is converted to an ASCII character with ANSI color codes. The
    character is chosen based on luminance, and colors are applied using 24-bit
    RGB ANSI escape sequences.




Buffer allocation is precisely calculated to avoid waste and
    prevent overflows:


    
  
  • Each pixel: 1 ASCII char + foreground ANSI code (19 bytes max)
    • 
  
  • Background mode: adds background ANSI code (19 bytes max per pixel)
    • 
  
  • Each row: reset sequence (\033[0m = 4 bytes) + newline (except last
    row)
    • 
  
  • At the end: null terminator (1 byte)
    • 



Color modes:


    
  
  • Foreground only (default): ASCII characters with colored foreground
    • 
  
  • Background mode (opt_background_color): colored background with
      contrasting foreground (black on bright backgrounds, white on dark
      backgrounds)
    • 



ANSI escape code format:


    
  
  • Foreground: \033[38;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Background: \033[48;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Reset: \033[0m (4 bytes)
    • 



Parameters


p Pointer to image_t structure containing
  pixel data


Returns


Dynamically allocated string containing colored ASCII
  art, or NULL on error. Caller is responsible for freeing the returned
  string.


Note


The function performs overflow checks to prevent integer
  overflow when calculating buffer sizes for very large images.



Uses global opt_background_color to determine color mode.




Exits with ERROR_BUFFER if buffer overflow is detected during
    string construction (should never happen with correct calculation).




Definition at line 195 of file foreground.c.


195                                                                {
196   if (!p || !palette) {
197     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
198     return NULL;
199   }
200   if (!p->pixels) {
201     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p->pixels is NULL");
202     return NULL;
203   }
204 
205   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
206   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
207   if (!utf8_cache) {
208     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar color rendering");
209     return NULL;
210   }
211 
212   const int h = p->h;
213   const int w = p->w;
214 
215   // Constants for ANSI escape codes (using exact sizes from ansi_fast.c)
216   const size_t max_fg_ansi = 19; // \033[38;2;255;255;255m
217   const size_t max_bg_ansi = 19; // \033[48;2;255;255;255m
218   const size_t reset_len = 4;    // \033[0m
219 
220   const size_t h_sz = (size_t)h;
221   const size_t w_sz = (size_t)w;
222 
223   // Ensure h * w won't overflow
224   if (h_sz > 0 && w_sz > SIZE_MAX / h_sz) {
225     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Image dimensions too large: %d x %d", h, w);
226     return NULL;
227   }
228 
229   const size_t total_pixels = h_sz * w_sz;
230   const size_t bytes_per_pixel = 1 + max_fg_ansi + max_bg_ansi; // Conservative estimate for max possible
231 
232   // Ensure total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel won't overflow
233   if (total_pixels > SIZE_MAX / bytes_per_pixel) {
234     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Pixel data too large for buffer: %d x %d", h, w);
235     return NULL;
236   }
237 
238   const size_t pixel_bytes = total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel;
239 
240   // Per row: newline (except last row)
241   // Final reset added once by ansi_rle_finish()
242   const size_t total_newlines = (h_sz > 0) ? (h_sz - 1) : 0;
243   const size_t final_reset = reset_len; // One reset at end (from ansi_rle_finish)
244 
245   // Final buffer size: pixel bytes + per-row newlines + final reset + null terminator
246   const size_t extra_bytes = total_newlines + final_reset + 1;
247 
248   if (pixel_bytes > SIZE_MAX - extra_bytes) {
249     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Final buffer size would overflow: %d x %d", h, w);
250     return NULL;
251   }
252 
253   const size_t lines_size = pixel_bytes + extra_bytes;
254   char *lines;
255   lines = SAFE_MALLOC(lines_size, char *);
256 
257   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
258   // char *current_pos = lines;
259   // const char *buffer_end = lines + lines_size - 1; // reserve space for '\0'
260 
261   // Initialize the optimized RLE context for color sequence caching
262   // Note: This function should be called via image_print_with_capabilities() for proper per-client rendering
263   ansi_rle_context_t rle_ctx;
264   ansi_color_mode_t color_mode = ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND; // Default to foreground-only for legacy usage
265   ansi_rle_init(&rle_ctx, lines, lines_size, color_mode);
266 
267   // Process each pixel using the optimized RLE context
268   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
269     const int row_offset = y * w;
270 
271     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
272       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
273       int r = pixel.r, g = pixel.g, b = pixel.b;
274       // Standard ITU-R BT.601 luminance calculation (uses original values for accuracy)
275       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
276 
277       // Use UTF-8 character cache for proper character selection
278       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
279       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
280 
281       // For single-byte ASCII, use RLE optimization; for multi-byte UTF-8, write directly
282       if (char_info->byte_len == 1 && (unsigned char)char_info->utf8_bytes[0] < 128) {
283         // ASCII character - use RLE optimization
284         const char ascii_char = char_info->utf8_bytes[0];
285         ansi_rle_add_pixel(&rle_ctx, (uint8_t)r, (uint8_t)g, (uint8_t)b, ascii_char);
286       } else {
287         // Multi-byte UTF-8 character - write color code and full UTF-8 bytes directly
288         int written = SAFE_SNPRINTF((char *)&rle_ctx.buffer[rle_ctx.length],
289                                    rle_ctx.capacity - rle_ctx.length,
290                                    "\033[38;2;%d;%d;%dm", r, g, b);
291         rle_ctx.length += written;
292         // Write UTF-8 bytes
293         for (int i = 0; i < char_info->byte_len && rle_ctx.length < rle_ctx.capacity; i++) {
294           rle_ctx.buffer[rle_ctx.length++] = char_info->utf8_bytes[i];
295         }
296       }
297     }
298 
299     // Add newline after each row (except the last row)
300     if (y != h - 1 && rle_ctx.length < rle_ctx.capacity - 1) {
301       rle_ctx.buffer[rle_ctx.length++] = '\n';
302     }
303   }
304 
305   ansi_rle_finish(&rle_ctx);
306 
307   return lines;
308 }










char
  * image_print_color_utf8 (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 312 of file foreground.c.


312                                                                     {
313   if (!p || !palette || !p->pixels) {
314     return NULL;
315   }
316 
317   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
318   if (!utf8_cache) {
319     return NULL;
320   }
321 
322   const int h = p->h;
323   const int w = p->w;
324   const size_t h_sz = (size_t)h;
325   const size_t w_sz = (size_t)w;
326 
327   if (h_sz > 0 && w_sz > SIZE_MAX / h_sz) {
328     return NULL;
329   }
330 
331   const size_t bytes_per_pixel = 30; // fg sequence (~19) + char (up to 4) + margin
332   const size_t total_pixels = h_sz * w_sz;
333   const size_t lines_size = total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel + h_sz + 16;
334 
335   char *lines;
336   lines = SAFE_MALLOC(lines_size, char *);
337 
338   char *ptr = lines;
339   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
340 
341   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
342     const int row_offset = y * w;
343 
344     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
345       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
346 
347       ptr += SAFE_SNPRINTF(ptr, 20, "\033[38;2;%d;%d;%dm", pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
348 
349       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
350       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
351       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
352       for (int i = 0; i < char_info->byte_len; i++) {
353         *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[i];
354       }
355     }
356 
357     *ptr++ = '\033';
358     *ptr++ = '[';
359     *ptr++ = '0';
360     *ptr++ = 'm';
361     if (y < h - 1) {
362       *ptr++ = '\n';
363     }
364   }
365 
366   *ptr = '\0';
367   return lines;
368 }






References rgb_pixel_t::b, utf8_char_t::byte_len,
    utf8_palette_cache::cache, rgb_pixel_t::g,
    get_utf8_palette_cache(), image_t::h, image_t::pixels,
    rgb_pixel_t::r, SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_SNPRINTF,
    utf8_char_t::utf8_bytes, and image_t::w.


Referenced by ascii_convert(), and
    image_print_with_capabilities().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>
